notes: Launched Sept 23 2003 — three weeks after the last Aug 2003 weekly; the architectural inflection missed by the commentary
32-bit processor recommended throughout the Aug 2003 weeklies
Server variant of K8/Hammer architecture; launched April 22 2003 — a visible 64-bit signal available when the Aug 2003 weeklies were written
The 64-bit extension to x86 that Athlon 64 introduced to consumers; adopted industry-wide and became dominant through 2010s
Intel's adoption of AMD's x86-64 — first shipped mid-2004 (Nocona Xeon), mainstream desktop 2005 (Pentium 4 6xx); confirms AMD-early thesis
Shipped April 25 2005 — 19 months after Athlon 64 launch; poor driver ecosystem limited adoption; OS support latency central to this memoir's thesis
The OS actually installed on every Athlon XP or early Athlon 64 consumer PC sold in 2003-2005
First consumer-facing 64-bit Windows with meaningful driver support; Jan 2007 launch — 40+ months after Athlon 64
